title: Record-breaking 5.6 Tbps DDoS attack and global DDoS trends for 2024 Q4
text: Cloudflare mitigated a record-breaking DDoS attack peaking at 5.6 Tbps during the week of Halloween 2024. Cloudflare's autonomous DDoS defense systems successfully detected and blocked a 5.6 Terabit per second (Tbps) DDoS attack, the largest attack ever reported. Cloudflare mitigated 21.3 million DDoS attacks in 2024, representing a 53% increase compared to 2023. Throughout the year, there were significant quarterly increases, with the first quarter of 2024 seeing Cloudflare's autonomous systems mitigate over 4.5 million DDoS attacks, a 50% increase compared to the previous year. Cloudflare mitigated nearly 6 million DDoS attacks in the third quarter of 2024, representing a 49% increase QoQ and 55% increase YoY. The fourth quarter of 2024 saw a surge in hyper-volumetric Layer 3/Layer 4 DDoS attacks. The growing use of powerful botnets, driven by geopolitical factors, has broadened the range of vulnerable targets. Additionally, a rise in Ransom DDoS attacks is also a growing concern for the industry.
